---
title: "NATO MRP Reweighting"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-02"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```

```{r}

country_avg_poll <- read.csv(here("output", "country_poll_nato.csv"))


country_avg_poll <- country_avg_poll %>%
  select(country, net_score_poll_cntry_avg = net_score)

# Load constituency auxiliary data (for region)
aux <- read.csv("https://mrp-psf.ams3.digitaloceanspaces.com/other-files/const_data_full_new.csv")
aux <- aux %>% mutate(const_name = gsub("Ynys Mon", "Ynys Môn", const_name))

# Create region_2 variable in aux
aux <- aux %>%
  mutate(
    region_2 = case_when(
      region_name %in% c(
        "East Midlands", "East of England", "Greater London",
        "North East England", "North West England", "South East England",
        "South West England", "West Midlands", "Yorkshire and the Humber",
        "London", "England"
      ) ~ "England",
      region_name == "Scotland" ~ "Scotland",
      region_name == "Wales"    ~ "Wales",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )


preds_nato <- read.csv(here("output", "nato_constituency_summary_revised.csv"))

# Join region onto predictions
preds_nato <- preds_nato %>%
  left_join(aux %>% select(const_name, region_2), by = "const_name") %>%
  mutate(
    region_2 = case_when(
      const_name == "Ynys MÃ´n" ~ "Wales",
      TRUE                      ~ region_2
    )
  )


preds_nato <- preds_nato %>%
  rename(net_score_preds = net_score)

preds_nato <- preds_nato %>%
  left_join(
    country_avg_poll %>% select(country, net_score_poll_cntry_avg),
    by = c("region_2" = "country")
  )

# Compute MRP average net score per country
preds_nato <- preds_nato %>%
  group_by(region_2) %>%
  mutate(mrp_average = mean(net_score_preds)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Additive adjustment: shift each constituency by the gap between
# the poll country average and the MRP country average

# Applies an additive adjustment to align MRP estimates with external poll margins,
# correcting for any systematic over- or under-estimation in the MRP predictions.
# For each category, the difference between the poll benchmark and the MRP national 
# average (e.g. poll_remain - mrp_avg_remain) is calculated and added uniformly 
# across all constituencies, shifting the entire distribution up or down by a 
# constant amount while preserving the relative geographic variation the MRP estimated.

# Additive adjustment is preferred over multiplicative here because it maintains the
# absolute differences between constituencies (e.g. a 10-point gap between two 
# constituencies stays a 10-point gap after adjustment), whereas a multiplier would 
# compress or inflate geographic variation unevenly — disproportionately affecting  
# constituencies with extreme values and distorting the pattern of regional differences
# the MRP was designed to recover. Additive adjustment also ensures probabilities 
# remain on a linear scale and are less likely to push boundary constituencies 
# below 0 or above 1 in implausible ways.


preds_nato <- preds_nato %>%
  mutate(
    adjustment           = net_score_poll_cntry_avg - mrp_average,
    net_score_reweighted = net_score_preds + adjustment
  )


preds_nato_reweighted <- preds_nato
write.csv(preds_nato_reweighted, here("output", "preds_nato_reweighted_revised.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
